data.content: Here's the conversion. With `MSTest.Sdk`, the three MSTest package references are replaced by the SDK itself:

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert from a standard MSTest v2 project to MSTest.Sdk. MSTest.Sdk simplifies the project file by:

1. Changing the SDK from `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` to `MSTest.Sdk`
2. Removing the three MSTest-related package references (`MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) since they're all included automatically by the SDK
3. You need to specify a version on the SDK itself

The MSTest.Sdk was introduced with MSTest 3.x. The current latest stable version is 3.7.x or so. 

I'll go with version 3.7.0 as a stable, well-established release that should work reliably.

  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Here's what changed:

1. **SDK** — `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` → `MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0` (the version pins MSTest)
2. **Removed all 3 package references** — `MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` are now provided automatically by the SDK
3. **Removed `IsPackable`** — MSTest.Sdk sets this to `false` by default

Your test code (`UserServiceTests.cs`) needs no changes — the same `[TestClass]`/`[TestMethod]` attributes work as before. You may want to check [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/packages/MSTest.Sdk) for the latest SDK version.
